evidence: R1 has a specific route for 172.16.0.0/24 pointing at R2: routing -> prefix 172.16.0.0 mask 255.255.255.0 fwd-list fwd: 10.0.0.2
R2 routing table shows only a default route: routing -> prefix 0.0.0.0 mask 0.0.0.0 fwd-list fwd: 10.0.0.6 (no route for 192.168.0.0/24 present)
R2 is directly connected to R1 on Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.2), so absence of a return route on R2 will cause R2 to forward traffic to its default rather than back to 192.168.0.0/24
R2 running-config 'routing' contains only a default route: "prefix": "0.0.0.0" -> "fwd": "10.0.0.6"; no entry for 192.168.0.0/24 (get_running_config for R2).

R1 has a route for 172.16.0.0 via 10.0.0.2 (R1 routing: "prefix": "172.16.0.0" -> "fwd": "10.0.0.2"), confirming forward path exists from 192.168.0.12 toward 172.16.0.12 but R2 lacks the reciprocal route.
previous_changes shows add_static_route on router2 with args: {"network": "192.168.0.0", "mask": "255.255.255.0", "next_hop": "10.0.0.2"}.
That request returned an error: {"status": "error","message": "Client error '400 Bad Request' ..."} (previous_changes result).

plan_history.intent_description: Source 192.168.0.12 cannot reach destination 172.16.0.12 because R2 has no return route for 192.168.0.0/24; after remediation, replies from 172.16.0.12 should route back to 192.168.0.12 and pings should succeed.
plan_history.problem: R2 lacks a route for 192.168.0.0/24, causing asymmetric routing/blackholing of replies from 172.16.0.12 to 192.168.0.12.
plan_history.fix_summary: Add a static route on router2 for 192.168.0.0/24 with next-hop 10.0.0.2 (R1) to restore the return path.

plan_history.intent_description: Source 192.168.0.12 cannot reach destination 172.16.0.12 because R2 has no return route for 192.168.0.0/24; after remediation replies from 172.16.0.12 should route back to 192.168.0.12 and pings should succeed.
plan_history.problem: Router2 has no route for 192.168.0.0/24, so return traffic from 172.16.0.12 is unroutable.
plan_history.fix_summary: Add a static route on router2 for 192.168.0.0/24 via next-hop 10.0.0.1 (assumed R1). Previous add_static_route failed because next_hop was incorrectly set to 10.0.0.2 (R2's own address).

remaining_issues: R2 access-list 'R2_TO_R1' contains an ACE that DENYs tcp from 172.16.0.0/24 to 192.168.0.0/24 dst-port 443 — this may block HTTPS traffic between those networks if relevant.
